Title: Glycosylated sterols enhance cold tolerance in tomato via membrane stabilization and jasmonate signaling

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Steryl glycosides (SG); indirectly associated with increased acylated steryl glycosides (ASG)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a glucose moiety to free sterols to form steryl glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Membrane stabilization and enhancement of cold tolerance through promotion of jasmonate signaling, antioxidant defenses, and cold-responsive gene activation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected in tomato; overexpressed in leaves of transgenic lines
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher transcript levels in leaves of SGT2ox lines under basal conditions
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plasma membrane-associated / endomembrane sterol metabolism, NA if exact localization not directly shown
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold stress at 4°C; basal non-stress conditions at 25°C
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases glycosylated/free sterol ratio, elevates jasmonates and putrescine, reduces membrane damage and oxidative stress, and enhances cold tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Constitutive overexpression of SlSGT2 increased SG and ASG and reduced free sterols under basal conditions. 2. SlSGT2 overexpression improved cold tolerance, lowered electrolyte leakage, preserved Fv/Fm and chlorophyll, and reduced ROS and MDA accumulation. 3. SlSGT2 overexpression was associated with stronger induction of cold-responsive genes, enhanced antioxidant enzyme activities, and higher JA, JA-Ile, and putrescine levels under basal and cold conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression; sterol profiling by GC-MS; cold tolerance phenotyping; electrolyte leakage; chlorophyll and Fv/Fm measurements; ROS staining; MDA assay; antioxidant enzyme assays; RT-qPCR; RNA-seq; jasmonate and polyamine metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No apparent impact on plant growth under non-stress conditions in overexpression lines
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising engineering target to increase tomato cold tolerance by elevating glycosylated sterol levels without obvious growth penalty; useful for metabolic engineering or transgenic/edited enhancement of membrane sterol glycosylation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper presents SlSGT2 as a causal driver of a higher GS/FS ratio that preconditions stress-responsive transcription and promotes jasmonate-associated cold acclimation.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a glucose moiety to free sterols to form steryl glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Membrane structural integrity and cold-stress protection
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected in tomato; reduced in amiRNA-silenced lines
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plasma membrane-associated / endomembrane sterol metabolism, NA if exact localization not directly shown
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold stress at 4°C; basal non-stress conditions at 25°C
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing decreases glycosylated/free sterol ratio, increases membrane damage and oxidative stress, and compromises cold tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlSGT1 silencing decreased SG and increased free sterol levels, lowering the GS/FS ratio. 2. SlSGT1-silenced plants showed dwarfism, higher electrolyte leakage, stronger wilting, lower photosynthetic performance, and greater ROS and MDA accumulation under cold stress. 3. SlSGT1 silencing was associated with weaker cold-responsive transcriptional and metabolic responses, including reduced jasmonate-related activation compared with SlSGT2 overexpression lines.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene silencing (amiRNA); sterol profiling by GC-MS; cold tolerance phenotyping; electrolyte leakage; chlorophyll and Fv/Fm measurements; ROS staining; MDA assay; antioxidant enzyme assays; RT-qPCR; RNA-seq; jasmonate and polyamine metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing causes dwarfism and enhanced cold sensitivity
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Avoid downregulation or loss-of-function of SlSGT1 in breeding because reduced glycosylated sterols compromise growth and chilling tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as an antagonistic genetic perturbation demonstrating that reduced GS/FS ratio is detrimental to cold acclimation.
